retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Evaluation -- Interpretation of Results
retrieved_docs.content: SD-OCT: will reveal parafoveal thinning of the photoreceptor integrity line and the outer nuclear layer of the retina; this results in a "flattening" of the foveal depression and the "flying saucer sign" where the outer nuclear layer in the fovea's center is unaffected, and just around it this layer is much thinner (the edge of the saucer).

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine bind to melanin in the retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) and cause damage to the macular cones outside of the fovea. The drugs inhibit the lysosome activity and reduce phagocytosis of shed photoreceptor outer segments causing an accumulation. In response, pigment-containing RPE cells migrate into the outer nuclear and outer plexiform layers of the retina, resulting in irreversible photoreceptor loss and RPE atrophy. [5] HCQ has a long half-life and takes about 6 months to achieve full elimination from the body. This is clinically significant when managing side effects such as retinal toxicity and explains continued maculopathy even after discontinuation of the medication. Corneal deposits result from binding to cellular lipids and deposition of the drug in the basal epithelial layer of the cornea. Discontinuation of the drug usually causes the deposits to disappear over time.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Evaluation -- Interpretation of Results
retrieved_docs.content: FAF: reveals in early maculopathy a ring of hyperfluorescence (caused by the accumulation of lipofuscin) and, in later stages, a ring of hypofluoresence (caused by the loss of photoreceptor and retinal pigment epithelial layers). [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: SD-OCT testing visualizes the retinal layers in as much detail as microscopic examination. In CQ/HCQ retinopathy, a loss of photoreceptor inner-outer segment junction (the ellipsoid zone or the photoreceptor integrity line (PIL)) and a thinning of the outer nuclear layer of the retina are observed. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Toxicity and Adverse Effect Management
retrieved_docs.content: Hemodialysis is not currently indicated or recommended in the management of hydroxychloroquine or chloroquine toxicity. [49] Of note, ophthalmic manifestations of toxicity are not typically seen in acute overdoses. [22] Vision and hearing changes are much more commonly seen with chronic use and tend to be irreversible. [50]

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Evaluation -- Interpretation of Results
retrieved_docs.content: The dilated fundus exam: 2016 recommendations indicate that end-stage bull's eye maculopathy presents less often with better dosing guidelines and earlier detection. However, the clinician should have familiarity with the fundus appearance of irregularity in the macular pigmentation in the early phase, a ring of macular pigment dropout in the advanced stage, and peripheral bone spicule formation, vascular attenuation, and optic disc pallor in the end-stage.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Evaluation -- Interpretation of Results
retrieved_docs.content: Visual field: Defects are most likely to occur at 5 degrees from the center, except in Asian patients where the defect may be over 10 degrees from the center. Use statistical analysis to determine the significance of the data.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Even when the clinician and patient adhere to screening guidelines with chronic use, discontinuing chloroquine or hydroxychloroquine therapy may not stop the progression of retinopathy to a stage where the patient's vision is spared.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: When the clinician who detects early retinopathy does not discontinue CQ/HCQ therapy, the prognosis is a progressive loss of paracentral vision followed by loss of central vision and night blindness. Even when the clinician discontinues therapy, retinopathy can progress for years after. Progression of vortex keratopathy causes increasing haloes and blur but is fully reversible upon discontinuation of therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: An ocular history should include specific questions regarding any preexisting retinal disease. Additionally, questions about central acuity and the ability to focus on near objects and glare should be asked. Common nonocular adverse effects of CQ and HCQ include pruritus, headaches, dizziness, and gastrointestinal upset, which should be asked about as they can lead to medication noncompliance. HCQ myopathy is uncommon but can present with proximal muscle weakness. In these cases, a muscle biopsy can determine characteristic pathologic findings. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Convincing evidence of chronic toxicity should result in the drug's discontinuation in consultation with the prescribing physician. Because of the long half-life of both drugs, retinopathy can continue for over 6 months, and studies have shown ongoing changes for up to 20 years after cessation of the drugs. [30] The goal is to detect early indications of retinal toxicity while the patient is still asymptomatic.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine -- Adverse Effects
retrieved_docs.content: Although chloroquine has relatively few side effects when taken as prescribed, higher doses of chloroquine have been shown to have severe adverse effects. The most severe adverse effects associated with high doses of chloroquine include retinal toxicity, long and subtle symptoms of reduced visual acuity, diplopia, and bilateral loss of vision. [11] High doses have also been shown to cause severe psychiatric issues, such as paranoia, hallucinations, and suicidal ideations. [12] Dermatological reactions include pruritus and photosensitivity. Retinopathy will typically present with the inability to focus between near and far objects. Neuropathy can include seizures, paranoia, and hallucinations.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Consultations
retrieved_docs.content: All hydroxychloroquine and chloroquine overdoses should be managed in consultation with a medical toxicologist or a local poison control center. In addition, patients prescribed HQ and CQ should be instructed on the importance of regular follow-up with ophthalmology starting at the onset of therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the treatment indications for chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine. Determine the mechanisms of action of chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine. Identify the presence of chloroquine or hydroxychloroquine toxicities in patients. Collaborate with the interprofessional healthcare team to implement appropriate management strategies in patients with chloroquine or hydroxychloroquine toxicity. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Differential Diagnosis -- The Differential for CQ/HCQ Retinopathy
retrieved_docs.content: Age-related macular degeneration: Geographic atrophy (GA) in the absence of drusen and choroidal neovascularization and CQ/HCQ retinopathy can present similarly. Both present with thinning of the photoreceptor integrity line and the outer nuclear layer of the retina and atrophy of the choriocapillaris on SD-OCT. [41] However, GA does not appear in a parafoveal ring (bull's eye) pattern. Conversely, especially in Asian patients, CQ/HCQ retinopathy does not present in the typical bull's eye pattern either, and the clinician will need to use other clinical criteria to differentiate the two conditions. One study revealed that reticular drusen are visualized in all patients with GA by SD-OCT and FAF, and their presence can be a valuable tool to differentiate GA from CQ/HCQ retinopathy. [42] mf-ERG reveals a typical paracentral reduction in CQ/HCQ retinopathy that is not present in GA.

retrieved_docs.title: Chloroquine and Hydroxychloroquine Toxicity -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: In malaria, chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine act as chemotherapeutic agents against erythrocytic forms of the Plasmodium parasites. Absorption of the drug increases the pH of the acidic food vacuoles of the parasite, which interferes with the reproduction of the parasite. [16] The drugs also inhibit parasite growth by interfering with the conversion of toxic heme, released from the parasite digestion of hemoglobin to the nontoxic hemozoin. [17] As a treatment for rheumatic disease, hydroxychloroquine and chloroquine increase the pH in the lysosomes of antigen-presenting cells and inhibit toll-like receptors (TLR) function on dendritic cells. It also reduces the activation of these cells and reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. [3] These drugs also accumulate in white blood cells and, by stabilizing lysosomal membranes, inhibit the activity of enzymes that cause cartilage breakdown, such as collagenase and protease. TLR 9 recognizes DNA-containing immune complexes, and inhibition by CQ/HCQ leads to inhibition of anti-DNA auto-inflammatory processes, such as in SLE. [18]

retrieved_docs.content: these conditions illustrate the complex interplay of inflammatory, vascular, traumatic, degenerative, and calcific mechanisms that can compromise retinal and choroidal integrity. Recognizing the full spectrum of disorders under the H30‑H36 classification aids clinicians in accurate diagnosis, appropriate monitoring, and targeted management to preserve visual function and address systemic associations where present.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 202
retrieved_docs.content: diabetes mellitus, its specific complications (various forms of retinopathy and nephropathy), and the relationship between these complications and ocular destructive procedures is essential for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2779
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Allowed qualifier” is connected to the pathophysiology of several distinct medical conditions, including diabetic kidney disease (اعتلال الكلية السكري), retinal detachment (انفصال الشبكية), threat of miscarriage (تهديد بالإجهاض), and fetal distress syndrome (متلازمة الضائقة الجنينية). Recognizing how the allowed qualifier relates to the underlying pathophysiological mechanisms of these disorders can help clinicians appreciate shared biological pathways and improve comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 2361
retrieved_docs.content: Headache; including migraine is classified as a condition that belongs to the broader category of Nervous system and sense organ diseases. The category Nervous system and sense organ diseases also includes the child entity اضطراب العين (eye disorder). Recognizing that both headache—including migraine—and eye disorders are subtypes of Nervous system and sense organ diseases helps clinicians organize related symptoms, consider shared pathophysiological mechanisms, and guide appropriate diagnostic and therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2634
retrieved_docs.content: (تنكس حصوي) and choroidal atrophy (تَنَكُّسُ المَشيمِيَّة), while localized calcification around the optic disc (حثل المشيمية حول الحُلَيمَة، الكلي) represents a specific form of mineral deposition. Finally, choroidal scarring (ندبة مشيمية شبكية) denotes fibrotic remodeling after injury or inflammation. Collectively, these entities underscore the choroidea’s central role in ocular disease, highlighting the need for careful clinical assessment and targeted management of choroidal involvement across diverse pathological contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: detachment of the eye (انفصال مشيمية العين) and hemorrhagic choroidal detachment (انفصال مشيمية العين النزفي). Peripheral involvement includes peripheral disseminated choroiditis with chorioretinitis and peripheral focal retinitis with retinochoroiditis. Across all entities, the choroidea (choroid) is the anatomical locus of the characteristic lesions, underscoring its central role in pathophysiology, clinical assessment, precise diagnosis, and tailored management of choroidal and retinal disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1178
retrieved_docs.content: postoperative choroïdoretinal scar (litteken; choroïdoretinaal, postoperatief), posttraumatic choroid scar, posttraumatic chorioretinal scar, and generic choroidal scar (litteken; choroidea). All of these entities illustrate that disorders of the choroid and retina involve inflammatory, infectious, vascular, degenerative, traumatic, sclerotic, calcific, detachment, and scar‑forming mechanisms, and that precise recognition of both English and Arabic terminology is essential for accurate classification, diagnosis, monitoring, and therapeutic planning in ophthalmic patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2169
retrieved_docs.content: Acute disorder of hemodialysis, sudden acquired retinal degeneration, and متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis dysfunction syndrome) are all described as having a clinical course characterized by sudden onset. The “sudden onset” qualifier indicates that the manifestations of these conditions develop rapidly, which is a key feature for clinicians to recognize when diagnosing and managing these disorders. Understanding that a rapid, abrupt presentation is common to these entities helps guide timely evaluation and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2128
retrieved_docs.content: taxonomy aids clinicians in recognizing the diverse presentations of choroidal and retinal pathology, facilitating accurate diagnosis, targeted management, and coordinated patient care across a range of ocular disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 194
retrieved_docs.content: and choroid. This hierarchical relationship clarifies the scope of ablative retinal therapies. Additionally, the disease referred to in Arabic as “داء ريفسام” manifests its clinical findings in the retina, reinforcing that retinal involvement—whether from systemic metabolic disorders, primary retinal diseases, or targeted retinal lesion destruction—requires precise identification of the retinal site for accurate diagnosis and treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 1 -- Community 266
retrieved_docs.content: and retina, leading to scarring, atrophy, detachment, neovascularization and visual impairment. Accurate identification of the specific choroidal and retinal pathology is essential for comprehensive evaluation, multidisciplinary management and optimal patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 1079
retrieved_docs.content: (isolated choroidal inflammation), التهاب المشيمية والشبكية (combined choroidal‑retinal inflammation), التهاب المشيمية والشبكية البؤري، غير محدد (focal unspecified combined inflammation), and حَثَلُ المشيمية الوراثي (hereditary choroidal atrophy). Collectively, these conditions demonstrate the complex interplay of vascular occlusion, hemorrhage, inflammation, metabolic injury, and degenerative remodeling within the retina and choroid; recognizing each as a child of “Disorders of choroid and retina (H30‑H36)” aids clinicians in systematic diagnosis, risk stratification, and the selection of targeted therapeutic strategies to preserve visual function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1709
retrieved_docs.content: The condition تمزق الشبكية (retinal tear) is directly linked to the need for repair of retinal tear; detachment, indicating that a retinal tear often requires surgical repair to prevent or treat detachment. This relationship highlights the importance of timely intervention for retinal tears to preserve visual function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 2 -- Community 599
retrieved_docs.content: is directly associated with retinal tear (تمزق الشبكية), indicating surgical repair as the treatment for this ocular injury. Collectively, these relationships illustrate a complex network in which chemical, embryologic, congenital, and treatment‑related qualifiers intersect across respiratory syndromes, cystic lesions, device complications, pain presentations, metabolic disorders, ophthalmologic and neurologic injuries, and vascular conditions, guiding nuanced diagnosis, preventive strategies, and therapeutic planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 1 -- Community 208
retrieved_docs.content: diabetes mellitus—all localized to the retina. The retina is also the primary site for retinal detachments, structural defects, vascular occlusions, and various other retinopathies. Therapeutic interventions targeting these lesions comprise destructive procedures on the posterior segment of the eye, categorized under “destruction of lesion of retina and choroid,” with radiation‑based destruction of localized retinal lesions (e.g., macular edema or tumors) as a further sub‑category. The disease داء ريفسام is likewise identified with retinal findings, underscoring the eye’s role in its manifestation. Clinical assessment employs the concept of “general clinical state” to interpret both biologic function failure and uncontrolled disease conditions; uncontrolled disease conditions are associated with diabetes mellitus with complications, highlighting the need for vigilant monitoring. Biologic function failure is linked to hepatic‑renal failure, indicating a critical organ‑system

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2602
retrieved_docs.content: Acute disorder of hemodialysis, First use syndrome of dialysis, Sudden acquired retinal degeneration, and متلازمة اختلال غسيل الكلى (dialysis dysfunction syndrome) are medical conditions that share a common clinical characteristic: a sudden‑onset course. In each of these disorders the disease manifests abruptly, indicating that their clinical course is defined by a rapid appearance of symptoms. Recognizing this sudden‑onset pattern is important for timely diagnosis and appropriate management of complications related to hemodialysis, initial dialysis use, retinal degeneration, or dialysis dysfunction.
